config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu, karena hak individu adalah batas agar mayoritas tidak sewenang-wenang terhadap orang kecil. Dalam pengalaman saya, alasan “demi masyarakat” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal solusi yang adil justru harus menyeimbangkan keduanya. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menelan hak pribadi, melainkan yang melindungi keduanya secara proporsional.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically come first over individual rights, because in the United States that idea has historically been used to excuse abuse by majorities and governments. Public safety matters, but rights like free speech, due process, and privacy are not optional conveniences; they are the guardrails that stop “the common good” from becoming a cover for overreach. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every case, since laws against fraud, violence, and public harm are necessary. For me, the right balance is not “society first” but “rights first, with limited exceptions when harm to others is real and clearly demonstrated.”

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text:
Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan dari hak individu. Di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang longgar, dari kebebasan berpendapat sampai perlakuan sewenang-wenang terhadap kelompok kecil, jadi saya tidak percaya mayoritas selalu lebih bijak. Hak individu justru penting sebagai pagar agar negara dan masyarakat tidak mudah mengorbankan orang yang lemah atas nama ketertiban.  

Tentu saja hak individu bukan berarti bebas melakukan apa pun; kalau tindakannya merugikan orang lain secara nyata, negara boleh membatasi. Tetapi prinsip dasarnya tetap harus melindungi individu dulu, lalu mencari titik temu dengan kepentingan sosial, bukan sebaliknya. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang adil bukan yang paling patuh pada mayoritas, melainkan yang paling serius menjaga hak setiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights in the general case, because in the United States that principle has too often been used to justify government overreach and majority rule that crushes dissent. Public safety and shared rules matter, but they should be narrow exceptions, not a blank check to override free speech, privacy, or due process whenever it is convenient. I also do not buy the idea that the “common good” is automatically wiser than the individual; history shows that crowds and institutions can be just as reckless as any one person. To me, a healthy society protects individual rights first and only limits them when there is a clear, direct harm to others.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Di Indonesia, dalih “demi ketertiban” atau “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan suara minoritas, jadi saya justru curiga kalau prinsip itu dijadikan patokan utama. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling cepat mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang berani membatasi kekuasaan mayoritas dan negara agar hak orang tetap aman. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa menjadikan hak individu sebagai korban default.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights in any broad, default sense. From an American perspective, that slogan has too often been a license for majorities, governments, and institutions to silence dissent, weaken privacy, or excuse unfair treatment in the name of “the common good.” Social order matters, but it is only legitimate when it is built around rights, with limits only for clear and direct harm to others. If we make individual rights secondary as a principle, then the people most likely to lose are usually the unpopular, the weak, and the minority.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
